Recovery Services vaults concentrated in eastus and using the RS0 SKU with infrastructure encryption enabled: because most vaults (68.9%) are in East US and RS0 with infra encryption is the tenant convention, provision matching Recovery Services vaults in the West region (RS0, infra encryption) and align identity models so ASR/backup artifacts and encryption settings are compatible during failover and failback.

Traffic DNS routing is a tenant convention (294 global Traffic Manager profiles) so DNS-based failover is already used: therefore implement automated DNS failover/failback using Traffic Manager or Front Door APIs and mirror the existing monitor configurations when switching endpoints to avoid unexpected probe/health behaviour.

Private DNS is heavily used (74 private DNS zones and 72 virtual network links): because internal name resolution relies on Private DNS zone links, pre-provision matching private DNS zones and virtual network links (or conditional forwarding) in the West region so internal services keep resolvable immediately after failover and during automated DNS rollback.

Virtual machines overwhelmingly have both system- and user-assigned identities (98.8%): since managed identities are the tenant pattern for service access, grant the same user-assigned identities required RBAC in the West region and use them for recovery automation runbooks and ASR permissions to avoid credential changes during failover.

Disks skew to Premium_LRS and are colocated with VMs (Premium_LRS is 63.2% of disks, and many disks are in eastus/westus2): because workloads expect premium storage performance, ensure the West target region supports equivalent Premium managed disk SKUs and pre-provision appropriate storage in the target region for replicated VMs.

A substantial share of storage accounts are single-region LRS (Standard_LRS 48.5%) while others use geo redundancy (Standard_GRS 23.3%, Standard_RAGRS 17.2%): given that many datasets are not geo-redundant, identify critical storage used by replicated VMs and convert or replicate those to geo-redundant storage or plan explicit cross-region replication prior to failover.

Virtual network topology shows hub/spoke peering names and eastus preference (VNet peerings like AppGWtoAKSVnetPeering and eastus 22.4%): because the tenant uses peered hub/spoke networks, include recreation of the hub, spoke VNets and peerings (or pre-provision a West hub) in the recovery plan to restore service connectivity and route dependencies after failover.

Networking and gateway resources use Regional Standard SKUs for public connectivity (Standard Public IPs and Standard Load Balancers are dominant): since production networking is based on Standard/Regional SKUs, pre-create equivalent Standard public IPs and load balancer resources in West and ensure health probes and frontend configurations match to enable smooth traffic cutover and automated DNS failbacks.
